source_formulae: P0R04728: 1. The Cytoskeleton: The L1 Quantum Scaffold
P0R04729: The cytoskeleton is the deepest layer of the interface, the substrate for L1 dynamics.

P0R04730: Microtubules (MTs): Hollow cylinders composed of - and -tubulin dimers. Dipole Moment: Tubulin dimers possess a large electric dipole moment (mu1750 Debye). | The L1 QEC Mechanism: The aligned dipoles within the helical lattice generate a strong interaction energy. J=40r1r32mu2 With spacing r8 nm, and effective dielectric constant r8.41, the screened coupling J0.82 eV. This forms the basis for the Topological QEC code, resulting in a large energy gap (Delta=2J1.64 eV). | Thermal Stability: This gap (Delta/kT61.4) protects quantum coherence against thermal noise (kT0.0267 eV), enabling coherence timescales relevant for neural processing. | Frhlich Condensation: MTs support collective vibrational modes (phonons) driven by metabolic energy, leading to long-range coherence. | Actin Filaments: Primarily located in dendritic spines. Regulate plasticity (L3). Actin dynamics may be modulated by quantum tunnelling (L1). | The Tensegrity Network: The cytoskeleton forms a tensegrity structure, transmitting mechanical signals (phonons), coupling L1 vibrations to L3 genomic regulation and L2 membrane activity.
